chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 1138 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.6778/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection at Flag-A 3. For orders on MA No.6779/2025 (Ex./A) 4. For hearing of bail application 25.11.2025 Mr. Ubedullah Kalwar, Advocate along with applicants / accused..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is allowed. 2. As to office objection, learned Counsel contends that despite this being a protective bail application, he shall comply the same within seven days. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 4. Applicants / accused Sheeraz Khan S/o Ameer Khan and Shah Nawaz S/o Hadi Bux Mangnejo seek protective bail in Crime No.01/2025, registered at Police Station CTW/FIA, Karachi, under Sections 406, 419, 420, 468, 471, 109, PPC, read with Sections 3 & 4 of Anti-Money Laundering Act, 2010, read with Section 11-N of Anti-Terrorism Act, 1997. It is, inter alia, contended that applicants / accused are prepared to surrender before the trial Court, but apprehend their arrest as NBWs have been issued against them. Without touching merits of the case, protective bail is granted to the applicants / accused for a period of seven days, subject to their furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty thousand) each and P.R. bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this Court. Operation of the NBWs shall stand suspended for the period of seven days. Applicants / accused shall surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period. This order shall cease to have any effect after expiry of seven days or when the applicants / accused surrender before the trial Court, whichever occurs earlier.

Applicants / accused shall surrender before the trial Court in the aforesaid period. This order shall cease to have any effect after expiry of seven days or when the applicants / accused surrender before the trial Court, whichever occurs earlier. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E Abdul Basit
